description: Character creation (sprint): map/species/background newgame-choice pushes, then ! -> newgame-random-combo
captured_from: https://crawl.dcss.io on 2026-07-03 (game_client content scrubbed, chat/spectator frames dropped)

messages.title:
<brown>Welcome, crawler.
<cyan>
You have a choice of maps:

labels: <lightgrey> b - Sprint II: "The Violet Keep of Menkaure"
<lightgrey> c - Sprint III: "The Ten Rune Challenge"

messages.title: the Conjurer
messages.doom_desc:
A malevolent fate is gathering around you. If your Doom reaches 100%, you will be afflicted by a random bane and your Doom will reset to 0. Banes have diverse negative effects that can significantly endanger your character for an extended period of time.

Both Doom and Banes are only cured by gaining experience, though Banes typically last much longer.

buttons.description: Gnolls are canine-like humanoids. They are unable to specialise in any single skill, but instead gradually become proficient in all skills at once.
Humans are natural explorers. Discovering new places refreshes and invigorates them. They are otherwise completely average.
Coglins ride enchanted exoskeletons able to wield weapons in both hands, wrapping slow-revving metal around their puny goblinoid frames.
Minotaurs are large muscular humans with bovine heads. They excel at all forms of close and ranged combat.
Kobolds are attuned to the shadows and can only see or be seen at a close range. They are small and a little fragile, and are talented in most skills.
Frail symbionts, Vine Stalkers cannot regain health from potions. Their magical reserves absorb damage, and they pack a magic-restoring bite.
Half fish, half human, Merfolk are citizens of both water and land. They are strong combatants, and adept at using magic to assist in battle.
Revenants are skeletal undead who are strong at melee combat and can empower damage-dealing spells by consuming memories they harvest from their victims.
Formless ghosts who delight in hexing their enemies. They can equip many different hats, gloves, boots, or cloaks at once, but cannot wear body armour.
Made of living stone, Gargoyles have a preternatural affinity for earth magic. They have low health, but their stone bodies are incredibly tough.
Demonspawn are half human, half demon. They acquire strong demonic mutations as they grow.
Demigods refuse to worship any god, but make up for this with divine attributes and high reserves of health and magic.
Mountain Dwarves are strong melee fighters and capable heavy-armoured casters. They can use scrolls to enchant even some artefacts.
Djinn are ever-burning spirits. They learn spells automatically as they grow, and cast by drawing down their own fires.
Formicids are humanoid ants adept at digging. Their limbs are exceptionally strong and they live in a state of permanent stasis.
Draconians are versatile hybrids. They mature when reaching level 7 and develop a colour.
Very small, very frail and very fast, Spriggans are highly competent stabbers and spellcasters.
Nagas are hybrids: human from the waist up, with a snake-like tail instead of legs. They move very slowly but are naturally resilient.
Trolls are monstrous creatures with powerful claws. They regenerate health rapidly.
Tengu are bird-people who love to fight, both with weapons and spells. They are fragile but agile, and eventually learn to fly.
Octopodes are a species of amphibious cephalopods. They can wear eight rings, but almost no armour fits them.
Deep Elves have a strong affinity for all kinds of magic, but are very fragile. They regenerate magical power rapidly.
Large, robust, and extremely fond of drinking, Oni make capable brawlers and spellcasters and gain extra benefits from healing potions.
Felids are many-lived sentient cats. They cannot use armour or weapons.
Scaled mammals the size of horses, Armataurs are fast-rolling and tenacious when pursuing enemies, but lack dexterity.
Barachim are slow-moving, amphibious humanoids who can hop long distances. Their extended range of vision also allows them to be seen from further away.
Compelled to walk by an ancient curse, Mummies have innate faith and are adept at Necromancy, but learn other skills very slowly. They cannot drink potions.
Picks a random recommended background based on your current species choice.

buttons.description: Fighters are equipped with armour and shield, as well as a weapon of their choice and potions of might.
Artificers start with a few magic wands and a club. They'll need to find better weapons and armour before their wands run out.
Hedge Wizards are magic generalists, good at using a wide variety of tools to frustrate their foes and gain an edge in the battlefield.
Gladiators are ready for the arena with light armour, a weapon of their choice, and a few throwing weapons and nets.
Shapeshifters use talismans to shift their form, making them powerful melee fighters (especially when unarmed) at the cost of armour.
Conjurers begin with the Magic Dart spell, and can learn other non-elemental spells to reliably blast their foes.
Monks start with a simple weapon of their choice, an orb of light, and a potion of divine ambrosia, and gain additional piety with the first god they worship.
Wanderers start with random equipment and skills.
Summoners are able to cast Summon Small Mammal from the start, and begin with spells to summon stronger and more exotic allies in their library.
Hunters carry a shortbow, light armour and a scroll of butterflies.
Necromancers start out with the Soul Splinter spell. Eventually, they can turn the dead into allies and use the life force of the living to restore themselves.
Brigands carry poisoned darts and a small number of nasty curare darts.
Forgewrights are mages who specialise in the creation of turrets and mechanical companions to fight alongside.
Warpers start with a weapon of their choice, a scroll of blinking, darts of disjunction, and some Translocation spells in their library.
Fire Elementalists have learned the Foxfire spell. Their other starting spells are likewise focused on fiery and explosive destruction.
Berserkers believe in Trog, the magic-hating god of frenzy. Not much can stop a raging berserker early on, apart from hubris.
Hexslingers carry a sling, and use hex magic to make their foes more vulnerable to attacks from afar.
Ice Elementalists begin with the Freeze spell. Their later spells provide strong offence and defence, but may restrict their movement.
Cinder Acolytes worship Ignis, drawing upon the flame of a dying god until there is nothing left. They have both melee and fire magic capabilities.
Enchanters know a variety of Hexes that can incapacitate their foes, and are equipped with an enchanted dagger and potions of invisibility.
Air Elementalists have learned the Shock spell. Their electrical magic can harm groups of foes, while their air spells assist in fighting or fleeing enemies.
Chaos Knights are playthings of the unpredictable Xom, and are subject to the god's constantly changing moods.
Reavers start with a weapon of their choice and deadly magic to supplement it when in need. They start with the Kiss of Death spell.
Earth Elementalists know the Sandblast spell, and can later learn more earthen spells suitable for subtle stabbing or brute-force blasting.
Alchemists start with the Poisonous Vapours spell, and their magic is very effective against foes susceptible to poison.
